ValueError: The features [4] have missing values in transform but have no missing values in fit.
agent_reasoning: **Strategy:** I dropped the tree-redundant `PolynomialFeatures` and `KBinsDiscretizer` layers that create noisy, high-dimensional inputs for LightGBM, replaced the slow `KNNImputer` with median imputation paired with `MissingIndicator` flags to preserve the signal of missingness, and added dataset-agnostic pandas string-meta features (length, word count, digit count) together with rare-category grouping—allowing the model to exploit raw text structure and high-cardinality columns without overfitting.

The `MissingIndicator` is configured with `features='missing-only'`, so it only tracks columns that contain NaNs during `fit`. If a numeric column happens to be complete in a training fold but has missing values in the validation fold (or test set), the transformer throws a dimension mismatch error. Setting `features='all'` guarantees that the indicator matrix always has the same number of columns as the input, regardless of where the NaNs appear.

I will improve CV by adding dataset-agnostic text-derived numeric features (frequency counts, first-character categories, digit flags, and extracted numbers) and enriching only the original numeric columns with KNN imputation, quantile binning, and low-order polynomial interactions, while keeping derived numeric features simple to avoid dimensionality explosion.

I will prune noisy derived numerical features (those that are >95% missing or constant) before modeling, insert a `SelectPercentile` feature-selection step after the preprocessor to drop the lowest-signal expanded features, and heavily regularize LightGBM (subsample, colsample_bytree, reg_lambda, more trees) to combat overfitting from the high-dimensional preprocessing pipeline.
